samples.xml_description:
<module id="multi_pipe_8bit">
    <description>Implement an unsigned 8bit multiplier based on pipelining processing.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset.</input>
        <input id="mul_en_in">Input enable.</input>
        <input id="mul_a" width_description="[7:0]">Multiplicand.</input>
        <input id="mul_b" width_description="[7:0]">Multiplier.</input>
        <output id="mul_en_out">Output enable.</output>
        <output id="mul_out" width_description="[15:0]">Product.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <param id="N">8</param>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
        <para>Reset mul_en_out_reg to 0 on the negative edge of rst_n.</para>
        <para>On the positive edge of clk, mul_en_out_reg gets the value of mul_en_in.</para>
        <para>mul_en_out is the most significant bit of mul_en_out_reg.</para>
        <para>Reset mul_a_reg and mul_b_reg to 0 on the negative edge of rst_n.</para>
        <para>On the positive edge of clk, if mul_en_in is 1, mul_a_reg and mul_b_reg get the values of mul_a and mul_b, respectively.</para>
        <para>Initialize temp with 8 wires of size [7:0].</para>
        <para>For each i from 0 to 7, assign temp[i] to the bitwise AND of mul_a_reg[i] and mul_b_reg.</para>
        <para>Reset sum to 0 on the negative edge of rst_n.</para>
        <para>For each i from 0 to 7, on the positive edge of clk, if i is 0, assign sum[0] to temp[0], otherwise, assign sum[i] to the sum of temp[i] and sum[i-1].</para>
        <para>Reset mul_out_reg to 0 on the negative edge of rst_n.</para>
        <para>On the positive edge of clk, assign mul_out_reg to the sum of all sum registers.</para>
        <para>On the positive edge of clk, if mul_en_out is 1, assign mul_out to mul_out_reg, otherwise, assign mul_out to 0.</para>
    </implementation>
</module>
